capture.source: Normalized from the preserved stdout of the exact checked-in runner execution. Numeric values and model output are copied without rerunning or estimating missing measurements.

fixture.reference_text: however due to the slow communication channels styles in the west could lag behind by 25 to 30 year

model.representation: Float16 Whisper Tiny tensors, Float32 80-bin mel filter, official multilingual tokenizer
model.license: MIT source model and official converter; the pinned converted model card has no structured license field

runtime.boundary: Rust f32 PCM and owned JSON through a repository-owned task-level C ABI to the official MLX C++ core

result.text: However, due to the slow communication channels, styles in the West could lag behind by 25 to 30 years.

segments.text: However, due to the slow communication channels, styles in the West could lag behind by

cpu.compute_dtype: Float32 activations with converted Float16 weights

reference_validation.implementation: mlx-whisper 0.4.3 using the pinned official MLX Python path as a test-only oracle
reference_validation.observation: The C++ adapter's final boundary is 100 ms later because it recomputes the complete decoder context instead of using the reference FP16 KV cache path.

observations: All six CPU and all six GPU transcriptions returned the same text, tokens, and segment boundaries.
The median of the three second GPU inferences was 115.811 ms, for an RTF of 0.010966950757575759.
The first-process load value is not a clean-host cold-start claim because system and Metal caches were not cleared.
No alternating powermetrics capture was made for this run.
The adapter exposes only task-level create, describe, transcribe, destroy, and free-string operations; no MLX type crosses the ABI.
